title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of May 31, 2026
content:
Portfolio Composition
Total member shares stood at $48,291,158 as of May 31, 2026, reflecting a year-over-year decline of -4.0%. The overall contraction was driven by reductions across all major share categories.
Regular Shares represented the largest funding component at $27,627,411 or 57.2% of total shares. Share Certificates followed as the second-largest category with a balance of $11,682,713 and a weighting of 24.2%. IRA/KEOGH Accounts comprised 14.8%, while Share Drafts accounted for 3.4%.
All major categories experienced declines: Regular Shares decreased by -3.5%, Share Certificates fell by -3.4%, IRA/KEOGH Accounts dropped significantly at -8.4%, and Share Drafts declined marginally by -1.0%. The only exception was All Other Shares, which grew 66.5% to $193,560 (0.4%). Because the largest components—Regular Shares and Share Certificates—all contracted simultaneously, the funding mix shifted toward a less flexible profile as fixed-term certificates remained significant while regular shares declined.
This period’s contraction in core share categories implies increased reliance on more stable but potentially higher-cost sources to offset volume losses. Management continues its focus on retaining member relationships and managing overall funding cost.
